reviews.review_description: I believe this game is one of the best sandbox games ever

reviews.review_title: Lot of potential, Lot of frustration
reviews.review_description:
I am quite conflicted on this game. It pains me to give it only 2 stars, but ultimately it comes down to implementation. On paper, the idea of the game is great.

Essentially, the main concept is this: An infinitely generated world where you can harvest resources and manipulate and cut them in real time. Materials have different strengths and some materials cannot damage or cut others that are stronger than them. But sharpness comes in to play. The thinner it is, the more of a knife like quality it has to chop. That's a very basic over view of materials.

The second thing is that the materials interact with the physics engine directly. Unlike most crafting games where you learn a recipe and you just put the materials into a crafting table and it produces an item, you manually connect the materials together yourself. This means you build a boat propeller from scratch, along with mounting the motor, building the hull, etc. You're not building off a blueprint or recipe, so you're working through the design choices yourself.

The huge frustrations come from lack of in-game tutorials/guidance, and abysmal handling. If you accidentally walk into a material you had on the ground, it can go flying through the air and get lost. You may go to take one piece that's bonded to another material, and more things fall apart that you have to try to place perfectly again.  Trying to build things and align them is extremely tedious, which makes it take forever to try to build a design you want to experiment with.

If you're trying to learn what materials work best together (i.e. how heavy is too heavy for a plane to get in the air), it can take forever to try to learn things. It does also feel like to even build an airplane for example, it basically has to weigh nothing.

It still deserves merit as how you break and chop materials is quite gratifying, the freedom is great. But as it stands now, I just found the juice wasn't worth the squeeze.
I got the game yesterday and wdym there's electricity and logistic gates and nerdy crap, PEAK ASF

This game is incomplete yes,but it is really cool to go around and make stuff like guns,cars,and houses,and it’s relaxing to play 

Cons 
Not the greatest water physics 
Can’t fly in creative or spawn in any items
Enemies have to much hp

Pros
Relaxing
Nice to make custom stuff 
Great potential 
Great machine physics 
Cool to fly

This is the kinda VR game you dream of. Pretty basic, considering the limitations of technology, but this is the most incredible freedom of realism (functionally) I've ever seen. It's incredible how you can chop down trees and actually make things like you would IRL. As if that wasn't cool enough, there's a vast array of ancient machinery you can find and build. This game legitimately has wind and water physics. If you make a twirly propeller or wheel, it can actually propel you in water or lift you off the ground.

It's incredibly fun how there's no "crafting", you legitimately have to cut every piece yourself and put together whatever you want, and it all reacts to physics. Rocks and wood split exactly as they would IRL. Even if just cubes, I get addicted to this game for days on end.

It does have a handful of glitches, but for what it is, that's kind of expected and doesn't ruin the gameplay in the slightest.

I legitimately forget this is a hidden AppLab game, this belongs on the top charts.
I like games like this, sandbox!

Love this game and love the mechanics, but there should 100% be a survival mode with this game, it gives a reason to eat and build to survive the weather or mosters, just make it a option when you start a world

I like the semi realistic crafting and building methods inside of the game, and as far as improvements go, I have none! Most of the things I would have asked for have been requested inside the discord already (which is a huge help) and the community is awesome, as people know the game so well and share their knowledge with others to overall better the game. Overall one of the top tier games on the quest store!
The sheer freedom of creation you have is astounding, the world is vast, with the only limit being your understanding of it.

Only put a half an hour or so into it but i like what i've seen so far. Seems to run really well and looks like it could be fun! Definitely looking forward to learning how it works and put more time into it. Hopefully it keeps getting updated.
It’s fun and the crafting is great, I love how it has shadows to, the enemies are interesting and the building is insane so is the physics, it’s like being an engineer.

One of the most fun game i played. You can create vehicles and mechanical things out of wood. You can also create houses and other carpentry stuff! You get to explore a large world looking for resources and get to fight drones and slimes. Or make a boat and sail the sea!

I really hope the dev continue to update this game with more content. It's really fun game so far and something i always wanted to exist.

I've had the game for about a week, and have played for several hours; here are my thoughts.

It's super fun! The ability to manipulate things freely in several ways is something I haven't seen in vr yet. Most of the enemies are slimes, and you can split them in half. The visuals are stylized, and it's something I really like about the game. The physics in this game are probably the best I've seen yet, except for if you try to touch several small free-moving parts together. The community is small, but so far very nice

Now the bad: it takes FOREVER to build pretty much anything complex. I started making a treehouse, but gave up after 2 days (I was also pretty busy over those days). The game is well optimized, but ONLY for large pieces. I made a houseboat, and have been wiring the electric motors up, but all of those small 1-pixel-wide pieces of iron really started to lag my game. The optimization is still pretty good besides that. There is a lack of enemies, lack of a set goal, and I can see it getting pretty repetitive pretty soon. 

If the dev/devs could polish the game and add more content, I can see this being my go-to survival VR game. As it stands, it's still very fun!
Very cool game, clearly not finished. Game has insane potential but needs easier controls and a more in depth help menu for tips on how certain things work
